---
title: "hwmon / pwmconfig problem - not persistent configs"
date: 2009-12-20
forum: General Help
---

### Post by Smane on 2009-12-20
Hi all ubunthusiasts! :) I've been using ubuntu for a few years now, and been able to solve almost any problem I have encountered because of all you helpful people at these forums. :) However now there is something which I have not found a solution.

I have installed lm-sensors and have ran pwmconfig to set my cpu fan speed.

So, after I ran pwmconfig it wrote to /etc/fancontrol/ this:

```
# Configuration file generated by pwmconfig, changes will be lost
INTERVAL=5
FCTEMPS=hwmon1/device/pwm2=hwmon1/device/temp2_input
FCFANS= hwmon1/device/pwm2=hwmon1/device/fan2_input
MINTEMP=hwmon1/device/pwm2=45
MAXTEMP=hwmon1/device/pwm2=60
MINSTART=hwmon1/device/pwm2=110
MINSTOP=hwmon1/device/pwm2=105
MINPWM=hwmon1/device/pwm2=0
MAXPWM=hwmon1/device/pwm2=255
```

Everything fine until now. Fancontrol was up and running, and I reduced the noise as I wanted. BUT THEN, after reboot it doesn't work. My cpu fan runs again at full speed. I have to manually change the /etc/fancontrol/ to this:

```
# Configuration file generated by pwmconfig, changes will be lost
INTERVAL=5
FCTEMPS=hwmon0/device/pwm2=hwmon1/device/temp2_input
FCFANS= hwmon0/device/pwm2=hwmon1/device/fan2_input
MINTEMP=hwmon0/device/pwm2=45
MAXTEMP=hwmon0/device/pwm2=60
MINSTART=hwmon0/device/pwm2=110
MINSTOP=hwmon0/device/pwm2=105
MINPWM=hwmon0/device/pwm2=0
MAXPWM=hwmon0/device/pwm2=255
```

So I change the hwmon1 to hwmon0 and restart fancontrol, and it works again. But after a reboot again, I have to change again hwmon0 back to hwmon1. So it is not persistent.

my /etc/modules/ looks like this: 

```
# /etc/modules: kernel modules to load at boot time.
#
# This file contains the names of kernel modules that should be loaded
# at boot time, one per line. Lines beginning with "#" are ignored.

lp

# Generated by sensors-detect on Tue Dec  8 17:24:00 2009
# Chip drivers
w83627ehf
k8temp
```

btw, it doesn't change at every reboot. It doesn't follow any pattern, it may work 3 or 4 reboots, but always at some point the change happens.

What could be the problem? I know this is not a big problem, but still annoying.

Thank You in advance!

---

### Post by dcstar on 2009-12-20
> **Smane said:**
> 
.......
What could be the problem? I know this is not a big problem, but still annoying.


The kernel may be incorrectly identifying your hardware on some reboots.

You need to report this as a bug:

```
ubuntu-bug pwmconfig
```

---

### Post by Smane on 2009-12-20

edit: thanks for a quick reply!

---

### Post by chung on 2010-01-10
I've found a workaround. Since the module k8temp is not necessary, we can blacklist it.

1. Remove the line k8temp in /etc/modules.
2. In the file /etc/modprobe.d/blacklist.conf, add the line "blacklist k8temp"

It should work.

---

### Post by guckpup on 2010-01-14
> **dcstar said:**
> The kernel may be incorrectly identifying your hardware on some reboots.

You need to report this as a bug:

```
ubuntu-bug pwmconfig
```

This behaviour has been [reported]("https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/linux/+bug/418246/comments/20") as part of "it87 (sensors) module broken, needs automatic adding of a kernel line during installation - [bug #418246]("https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/linux/+bug/418246")"

I'm hoping that the blacklist of the k8temp will help quell the insanity a touch.

---

### Post by guckpup on 2010-01-14
> **chung said:**
> I've found a workaround. Since the module k8temp is not necessary, we can blacklist it.

1. Remove the line k8temp in /etc/modules.
2. In the file /etc/modprobe.d/blacklist.conf, add the line "blacklist k8temp"

It should work.

Confirmed working on karmic 64 with sensors3.1.1!

I didn't have reference to k8temp in modules, but blacklisting it did the trick - now it87 is reliably hwmon2 and my asus ATK0110 acpi module is hwmon1.

Hooray! Thank you chung. Mods - consider switching topic to "solved"?

G

### Post by guckpup on 2010-02-21
Slight wrinkle since this blacklisting the k8temp. 

A new sensor "acpitz-virtual-0" has since appeared (and FWICT is as  use-free as the k8temp sensor) - I've read that I should be able to get  rid of it by adding "thermal" to the list of blacklisted modules.

No luck, unfortunately - my devices are back to erratically bouncing on  reboot (or not! Erratic!).

Since I've been unable to kill acpitz-virtual, I've instead at least  given myself a (pretty weak) way to quickly deal with it - a bash alias:

alias fixfans='diff /etc/fancontrol  /path/to/alternate_fancontrol_settings1 && sudo cp  /path/to/alternate_fancontrol_settings2 /etc/fancontrol || sudo cp  /path/to/alternate_fancontrol_settings1 /etc/fancontrol && sudo  /etc/init.d/fancontrol restart'

Since I've made and copied the two different 'path scenarios' I can use  the above to at least quickly "replace and restart with a working  fancontrol config" whenever the machine boots and sounds like it's about  to take off.

---
